regime.label: Geopolitical Pivot
regime.sublabel: Energy Shock Fading, Policy Fog Thickening
regime.description: Markets are navigating a sharp de-escalation in the Iran conflict, which drove oil from ~$67 to near $120 before pulling back toward $91–95 on ceasefire signals. Equity indices have recovered strongly from their late-March lows, but the macro backdrop remains cloudy: US CPI is sticky at 3.3%, the Fed is frozen at 3.50–3.75% with a hawkish split on its board, and a Fed Chair transition adds a new layer of institutional uncertainty. The CNN Fear & Greed Index at 47 (Neutral) and VIX at ~17 confirm markets are neither complacent nor panicked — a tentative equilibrium that could break decisively in either direction.

riskMeter.label: Cautiously Neutral
riskMeter.sublabel: Oil pullback and Iran deal hopes are lifting sentiment, but sticky inflation and Fed Chair uncertainty keep a ceiling on risk appetite.

spreads.label: 3M–10Y
spreads.note: Still inverted but narrowing sharply from -170 bps peak in late 2024; dis-inversion in progress.

spreads.label: 2Y–10Y
spreads.note: Mildly inverted; bear-steepening as long end rises faster on fiscal concerns than short end falls.

spreads.label: 5Y–30Y
spreads.note: Positive slope at the long end indicates term premium re-pricing amid elevated fiscal deficits.

yieldCurve.inversionStatus: 3M–10Y inverted for ~22 months; 2s10s briefly un-inverted Mar 2026, re-inverted on oil shock
yieldCurve.trajectory: Curve is bear-steepening — short end anchored by Fed hold while long end drifts higher on fiscal deficit concerns and term premium normalization; full un-inversion likely within 2–3 months if ceasefire holds.
yieldCurve.canadianCurve: BoC curve is already positively sloped (rate at 2.25% vs. 10Y GoC ~3.65%); ZAG.TO benefits if BoC resumes cuts in H2 — duration risk is manageable here compared to the US.

signals.label: S&P 500
signals.note: S&P had its best month since 2020 in April, recovering from Iran-shock lows; AI capex cycle and mega-cap earnings are the primary driver.

signals.label: TSX Composite
signals.note: TSX benefiting from elevated (though falling) oil prices; energy sector strength offsets financials drag. Outperforming EM but lagging S&P YTD.

signals.label: VIX
signals.note: Pulled back sharply from 28 peak in April as Iran ceasefire signals emerged; still above pre-conflict lows of ~13–14, reflecting residual uncertainty.

signals.label: Nasdaq / Tech
signals.note: AI infrastructure theme intact; mega-caps (NVDA, META, AMZN) reported solid Q1. Russell 2000 lagging, confirming large-cap quality bias.

signals.label: Fed Funds Rate
signals.note: Fed held for 3rd consecutive meeting on April 29; 8-4 vote split — most divisive decision since 1992. Markets pricing near-zero chance of cuts in 2026 as oil/inflation risks persist.

signals.label: 10Y US Treasury
signals.note: Pulled back from 9-month high of ~4.45% as Iran deal hopes crushed oil; still elevated and keeping valuation multiples under pressure.

signals.label: Yield Curve (2s10s)
signals.note: Still inverted but narrowing; bear-steepening driven by rising long-end term premium, not Fed cuts. Full un-inversion expected in 2–3 months.

signals.label: 10Y Real Yield
signals.note: Real yields at restrictive levels — compressing equity P/E multiples and capping gold upside; any Fed pivot would push real yields sharply lower.

signals.label: 10Y Breakeven Inflation
signals.note: Market pricing 2.35% average inflation over 10 years — slightly above Fed target but receding from 2.6% peak as oil retreats; consistent with one or two cuts by late 2026.

signals.label: BoC Rate
signals.note: BoC held April 29; projecting 1.2% GDP growth in 2026. CPI climbed to 2.4% in March on gasoline prices; core remains just above 2%. No cuts expected through mid-2026.

signals.label: US CPI (YoY)
signals.note: Inflation re-accelerated in Q1 on oil spike; now fading as energy prices pull back but services inflation remains sticky above 4%.

signals.label: US Unemployment
signals.note: Labour market resilient despite rate pressure; no meaningful deterioration yet — key Fed watchpoint for any pivot signal.

signals.label: Oil (WTI)
signals.note: Pulled back from ~$118 April peak on Iran ceasefire signals; still elevated vs. $67 pre-conflict. Direction of final deal is the key variable.

signals.label: Gold
signals.note: Holding near all-time highs; geopolitical uncertainty and central bank buying providing structural floor.

signals.label: USD/CAD
signals.note: CAD supported by oil but capped by BoC hold and weak domestic growth. Range-bound until oil direction clarifies.

signals.label: Fear & Greed
signals.note: Recovered from Extreme Fear (18) in late March; Neutral reading reflects ceasefire optimism offsetting macro uncertainty.

signals.label: AAII Bull/Bear
signals.note: Retail sentiment improving on equity recovery; not yet at levels associated with contrarian sell signals.

signals.label: Put/Call Ratio
signals.note: Declining from elevated March levels; near-neutral hedging demand confirms market is not in panic mode.

signals.label: Earnings Revisions
signals.note: Q1 earnings broadly beat low bar set by oil-shock fears; FY2026 estimates stabilizing after earlier downgrades.

scenarios.label: Bull case
scenarios.trigger: Iran deal finalised; oil falls to $70–75; CPI drops toward 2.8% — unlocking a Fed cut in September; yield curve fully un-inverts as 2s10s turns positive
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 35,000+; CAD weakens as oil slides but equities outperform on rate optimism; ENB, XIU benefit; ZAG.TO rallies on BoC cut bets
scenarios.positioning: Add quality growth (NVDA, AMZN, SHOP); deploy cash held back during oil-shock uncertainty; add ZAG.TO in RRSP on rate cut signal
scenarios.sectorRotation: Overweight Financials (steeper curve lifts NIM), Technology, Industrials; underweight Energy (falling oil), Utilities

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.trigger: Ceasefire holds but no final deal; oil stays $85–95; Fed stays on hold; AI earnings carry the index; yield curve continues gradual bear-steepening
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 32,500–34,500; CAD range-bound 1.37–1.40; energy names volatile around oil headlines; curve steepening is mildly positive for Canadian banks
scenarios.positioning: Hold diversified portfolio; DCA into weakness; keep 10–15% cash for H2 deployment window; maintain gold hedge
scenarios.sectorRotation: Neutral weight across sectors; mild tilt to Quality factor (large-cap, high-margin); reduce small-cap exposure given curve inversion risk

scenarios.label: Bear case
scenarios.trigger: Ceasefire collapses; oil spikes back above $110; CPI re-accelerates; Fed forced to hike; yield curve re-inverts deepening recession signal
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 29,000–31,000 — energy rallies but financials and rate-sensitives sold heavily; CAD strengthens briefly then reverses
scenarios.positioning: Lean into defensives (BRK.B, GOLD, RTX); increase ZAG.TO (short-duration); trim speculative tech positions; reduce leverage entirely
scenarios.sectorRotation: Rotate into Consumer Staples, Healthcare, Gold miners (GOLD, ABX.TO); avoid Financials (re-inversion crushes NIM), Real Estate, small-cap growth

keyEvents.event: FOMC meeting — rate decision and updated dot plot
Iran nuclear deal negotiation deadline window
Q2 earnings season — energy and tech sector readthrough

scenarios.label: Bull case
scenarios.trigger: Oil normalises below $75; 1–2 Fed cuts by year-end; AI investment super-cycle intact; new Fed Chair credible; yield curve fully normalised with 2s10s at +40–60 bps — historically bullish for equities 12 months forward
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 36,000+; BoC resumes cuts; housing stabilises; SHOP and Canadian tech rebound; ZAG.TO rallies 5–7%; GoC curve steepens
scenarios.positioning: Increase equity allocation to 80%+; add INDA, MELI for EM exposure; rotate some fixed income into equities; add XRE.TO as BoC pivots
scenarios.sectorRotation: Overweight Financials (steeper curve major tailwind for bank NIMs), Technology (rate-sensitive growth re-rates), Real Estate (XRE.TO); underweight Energy, Utilities

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.trigger: Geopolitical fog persists; Fed holds all year; growth moderate at 1.5–2%; margins hold on AI productivity; yield curve flat-to-slight-positive with 2s10s near 0–20 bps
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 32,000–35,000; CAD stable; XRE.TO and ZAG.TO outperform modestly as BoC eventually pivots; Canadian bank dividends provide buffer
scenarios.positioning: Stay the course; reinvest dividends; optimise TFSA/RRSP placement; add XRE.TO on weakness; maintain 10% gold allocation
scenarios.sectorRotation: Barbell strategy: Quality growth (NVDA, MSFT) + Defensives (staples, healthcare); underweight rate-sensitive cyclicals pending curve clarity

scenarios.label: Bear case
scenarios.trigger: New Fed Chair loses credibility; stagflation entrenches; credit spreads widen; corporate earnings fall 10%+; yield curve deepens re-inversion below -100 bps on 3M–10Y — recession probability spikes above 50%
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 26,000–29,000; housing correction accelerates; BoC forced into emergency cuts; GoC curve also inverts; CAD weakens sharply
scenarios.positioning: Max defensives; raise TLT + ZAG.TO to 20% of RRSP (cut duration plays benefit); hold GOLD ETF; reduce leverage entirely; build cash to 20%
scenarios.sectorRotation: Defensive maximum: Consumer Staples (L.TO, MRU.TO), Utilities (FTS.TO), Healthcare, Gold miners; liquidate all speculative growth, crypto, REITs

keyEvents.event: FOMC — potential first cut window if CPI cooperates
Q3 earnings — critical AI infrastructure capex readthrough
US midterm elections — fiscal policy uncertainty

bullish.label: Iran ceasefire deal finalised — oil drops to $70–75, CPI falls, clearing path for Fed cuts
Yield curve fully un-inverts (2s10s goes positive) — historically the strongest 12-month equity signal when paired with soft-landing data
AI capex super-cycle driving NVDA, MSFT, AMZN, GOOG earnings above consensus
Defense supercycle locked in by NATO 2% mandate regardless of Middle East outcome
Gold and CAD energy providing structural safe-haven ballast independent of rate cycle
GLP-1 and healthcare demographics driving secular demand for NVO, ISRG, LLY

bearish.label: Iran ceasefire collapse — oil back above $110 forces Fed to hike, yield curve deepens inversion, crushing equity multiples
Yield curve re-inversion below -100 bps on 3M–10Y — NY Fed recession probability spikes above 50%, triggering institutional de-risking
Fed Chair transition — new appointment seen as politically influenced, triggering bond sell-off and curve steepening for wrong reasons
Services inflation re-entrenching above 4% — last-mile problem proves unsolvable without recession
Commercial real estate stress spreading to regional bank loan books — credit event risk amplified by inverted curve compressing NIMs
US consumer credit card delinquencies at 10-year highs — spending slowdown accelerating, validating the curve's recession signal

portfolioImplication.summary: The Iran de-escalation is the single biggest variable — a confirmed deal unlocks a Fed pivot and a strong H2 rally. The yield curve's gradual bear-steepening is a cautiously positive sign (recession risk receding vs. peak inversion), but the 2s10s remains negative, warning against aggressive risk-on positioning. Until the curve fully un-inverts and CPI cooperates, maintain a cautious barbell: core quality growth + defensive hedges, with 10–15% cash ready to deploy.

actions.action: Hold GOLD and ENB — both performing their hedge function; yield curve inversion historically precedes commodity outperformance by 6–12 months
DCA into NVDA and AMZN on any 5–8% dips — AI capex is the one consensus trade that's still intact regardless of curve shape
Add ZAG.TO in RRSP as yield curve steepens — duration benefits if Fed eventually cuts; short-duration preferred over TLT until inversion fully resolves
Watch Canadian bank stocks (RY, TD) — they re-rate meaningfully when BoC curve steepens; the 2s10s un-inversion is the trigger
Review any TFSA position paying US dividends above 1.5% — WHT drag compounds painfully in a flat market
Watch SHOP and XRE.TO for entry points — both are cheap on a BoC-cut scenario but don't force it until curve fully normalises
